segments.text: Lesson 51.

translation.text: 第51课。

segments.text: Predicting the Future.

translation.text: 预测未来。

segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translation.text: 听录音，然后回答以下问题。

segments.text: What was the future electronic development that Leon Bagret wasn't able to foresee?

translation.text: 莱昂·巴格瑞特没能预见未来哪一项电子技术的发展？

segments.text: Predicting the future is notoriously difficult.

translation.text: 预测未来是出了名的困难。

segments.text: Who could have imagined, in the mid-1970s, for example, that by the end of the 20th century, computers would be as common in people's homes as TV sets?

translation.text: 例如，在20世纪70年代中期，谁能想到20世纪末电脑会像电视机一样普遍地进入家庭？

segments.text: In the 1970s, computers were common enough, but only in big business, government departments, and large organizations.

translation.text: 20世纪70年代，电脑已经相当常见，但只用于大企业、政府部门和大型机构。

segments.text: These were the so-called mainframe machines.

translation.text: 它们就是所谓的大型主机。

segments.text: Mainframe computers were very large indeed, often occupying whole air-conditioned rooms, employing full-time technicians, and run on specially-written software.

translation.text: 大型主机确实十分庞大，常常占据整间空调房，需要专职技术人员，并运行专门编写的软件。

segments.text: Though these large machines still exist, many of their functions have been taken over by small, powerful personal computers, commonly known as PCs.

translation.text: 这些大型机器虽然仍然存在，但许多功能已由体积小、性能强的个人电脑接替，也就是通常所说的PC。

segments.text: In 1975, a primitive machine called the Altair was launched in the USA.

translation.text: 1975年，美国推出了一台名为“牛郎星”的原始机器。

segments.text: It can properly be described as the first home computer, and it pointed the way to the future.

translation.text: 它完全可以称为第一台家用电脑，并指明了未来的发展方向。

segments.text: This was followed, at the end of the 1970s, by a machine called an Apple.

translation.text: 20世纪70年代末，随后又出现了一台名为“苹果”的机器。

segments.text: In the early 1980s, the computer giant IBM produced the world's first personal computer.

translation.text: 20世纪80年代初，电脑巨头IBM生产出世界上第一台个人电脑。

segments.text: This ran on an operating system called DOS, produced by a then-small company named Microsoft.

translation.text: 它运行一种名为DOS的操作系统，由当时还很小的微软公司开发。

segments.text: The IBM personal computer was widely copied.

translation.text: IBM个人电脑被广泛仿制。

segments.text: From those humble beginnings, we have seen the development of the user-friendly home computers and multimedia machines which are in common use today.

translation.text: 从那些不起眼的开端，我们见证了如今广泛使用、操作方便的家用电脑和多媒体设备的发展。

segments.text: Considering how recent these developments are, it is even more remarkable that as long ago as the 1960s, an Englishman, Leon Bagret, was able to predict some of the uses of computers

translation.text: 考虑到这些发展如此之新，更令人惊叹的是，早在20世纪60年代，英国人莱昂·巴格瑞特就能预见电脑的某些用途，

segments.text: which we know today.

translation.text: 也就是我们今天熟知的用途。

segments.text: Bagret dismissed the idea that computers would learn to think for themselves and would rule the world which people liked to believe in those days.

translation.text: 巴格瑞特摒弃了当时人们爱信的说法，即电脑会学会独立思考并统治世界。

segments.text: Bagret foresaw a time when computers would be small enough to hold in the hand, when they would be capable of providing information about traffic jams and suggesting alternative routes, when they would be used in hospitals

translation.text: 巴格瑞特预见有一天电脑会小到可以握在手里，能够提供交通堵塞信息并建议替代路线，还会在医院里使用，

segments.text: to help doctors to diagnose illnesses, when they would relieve office workers and accountants of dull, repetitive clerical work.

translation.text: 帮助医生诊断疾病，并把办公室职员和会计从枯燥、重复的文书工作中解放出来。

segments.text: All these computer uses have become commonplace.

translation.text: 电脑的所有这些用途如今都已司空见惯。

segments.text: Of course, Leon Bagret could not possibly have foreseen the development of the Internet, the worldwide system that enables us to communicate instantly with anyone in any part of the world by using

translation.text: 当然，莱昂·巴格瑞特不可能预见互联网的发展；这个全球系统使我们能通过

segments.text: computers linked to telephone networks.

translation.text: 连接电话网络的电脑，立即同世界任何地方的任何人交流。

segments.text: Nor could he have foreseen how we could use the Internet to obtain information on every known subject so we can read it on a screen in our homes and even print it as well if we want to.

translation.text: 他也不可能预见我们能利用互联网获取所有已知主题的信息，在家中的屏幕上阅读，愿意的话还可以打印出来。

segments.text: Computers have become smaller and smaller, more and more powerful, and cheaper and cheaper.

translation.text: 电脑变得越来越小、越来越强大，也越来越便宜。

segments.text: This is what makes Leon Bagret's predictions particularly remarkable.

translation.text: 正是这一点使莱昂·巴格瑞特的预测格外了不起。

segments.text: If he or someone like him were alive today, he might be able to tell us what to expect in the next 50 years.

translation.text: 如果他或像他这样的人今天还活着，也许能告诉我们未来50年会发生什么。
